reasoning: The 2025 National Heads-Up Poker Championship was pre-recorded in August 2025 and featured a 64-player field. Ryan Feldman was confirmed as one of the 64 players and drew Sean Winter in the first round. In mid-October 2025, there was a major leak on the Polymarket prediction platform that spoiled the pre-recorded winner. News reports identified Sam Soverel as the leaked winner, whose odds surged to 99%. Only three other players (Brian Rast, Erik Seidel, and Sean Winter) were reported to have odds above 1% at the time, leaving Ryan Feldman at virtually 0%. Because Sean Winter is listed among those who had >1% while Feldman does not, it is highly likely Feldman lost to Winter in the first round. Given the leak strongly points to Sam Soverel winning the entire tournament and Ryan Feldman not being among the final contenders, the probability of Ryan Feldman winning is extremely low. I will forecast 2%, largely to account for any negligible chance that the leak was an elaborate hoax or that the true winner was somehow misidentified in the reporting, but the evidence overwhelmingly points to a "No" resolution.
